hooks.prompt:
Review the git command that was just executed for safety issues. Check for:
1. Force push to main/master branch (git push --force origin main) — this rewrites shared history
2. Hard reset (git reset --hard) without first checking if there are uncommitted changes that would be lost
3. Amending a commit that has already been pushed — this rewrites published history
4. Deleting branches without checking if they're merged (git branch -D instead of -d)
5. Running git clean -f without --dry-run first — this permanently deletes untracked files
6. Rebasing commits that have been pushed to a shared branch
7. Using --no-verify to skip pre-commit hooks — this bypasses safety checks

For each issue found, explain the risk and suggest the safer alternative. Only flag actual dangerous commands, not safe git operations. Respond with {"ok": true} if the command is safe.
